2604.09443.title: Many-Tier Instruction Hierarchy in LLM Agents
2604.09443.paper_summary: Large language model agents receive instructions from many sources-system messages, user prompts, tool outputs, and more-each carrying different levels of trust and authority. When these instructions conflict, models must reliably follow the highest-privilege instruction to remain safe and effective. The dominant paradigm, instruction hierarchy (IH), assumes a fixed, small set of privilege levels (typically fewer than five) defined by rigid role labels (e.g., system > user). This is inadequate for real-world agentic settings, where conflicts can arise across far more sources and contexts. In this work, we propose Many-Tier Instruction Hierarchy (ManyIH), a paradigm for resolving instruction conflicts among instructions with arbitrarily many privilege levels. We introduce ManyIH-Bench, the first benchmark for ManyIH. ManyIH-Bench requires models to navigate up to 12 levels of conflicting instructions with varying privileges, comprising 853 agentic tasks (427 coding and 426 instruction-following). ManyIH-Bench composes constraints developed by LLMs and verified by humans to create realistic and difficult test cases spanning 46 real-world agents. Our experiments show that even the current frontier models perform poorly (~40% accuracy) when instruction conflict scales. This work underscores the urgent need for methods that explicitly target fine-grained, scalable instruction conflict resolution in agentic settings.
2604.09443.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型代理從多個來源接收指令——系統訊息、用戶提示、工具輸出等——每個來源都帶有不同的信任和權威程度。當這些指令發生衝突時，模型必須可靠地遵循最高特權的指令，以保持安全和有效。主導範式指令層級（IH）假設一組固定的小型特權層級（通常少於五個），由嚴格的角色標籤定義（例如，系統 > 用戶）。這對於現實世界的代理環境來說是不足夠的，因為衝突可能來自更多的來源和上下文。在這項工作中，我們提出了多層指令層級（ManyIH），這是一種解決具有任意多特權層級的指令衝突的範式。我們介紹了ManyIH-Bench，這是ManyIH的第一個基準測試。ManyIH-Bench要求模型在具有不同特權的衝突指令中導航多達12個層級，涵蓋853個代理任務（427個編碼和426個遵循指令）。ManyIH-Bench結合了由LLMs開發並由人類驗證的約束，以創建涵蓋46個現實世界代理的現實且困難的測試案例。我們的實驗顯示，即使是當前的前沿模型在指令衝突擴大時表現不佳（約40%的準確率）。這項工作強調了在代理環境中，明確針對細粒度、可擴展的指令衝突解決方法的迫切需求。
2604.09443.author: Jingyu Zhang et.al.
2604.09443.authors: Jingyu Zhang, Tianjian Li, William Jurayj, Hongyuan Zhan, Benjamin Van Durme, Daniel Khashabi
